title: ADOLESCENT AND FAMILY SERVICES - 211info
text: OREGON SOCIAL LEARNING CENTER ADOLESCENT AND FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers clinical and school-based support to adolescents with substance use and other life challenges. Utilizes the Contingency Management Protocol, a harm reduction approach to substance use. 10 Shelton McMurphey Boulevard Eugene, OR 974015414 Eligibility: Ages 12-18 who are enrolled in one of the 4J or Bethel School District High Schools, who have substance use or mental health concerns Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm (541) 485-2711 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 485-2711 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in FEES: None AREA SERVED: Lane County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Bus stop within 2 blocks Services: Comprehensive Outpatient Substance Use Disorder Treatment * Adolescents Other Services Offered At This Location: SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - T.H.E. VAN If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON SOCIAL LEARNING CENTER
